layoutPatterns.homepage: Large hero (3000x1500) + navigation banner (872x101) + wide product category tiles (2x872x436) + narrow category grid (8x436x218)

layoutPatterns.needStatePages: Hero banner (1872x400) + multiple product benefit sections with editorial images (14-16 images per page) + product grid
layoutPatterns.contentRichPages: Multiple editorial sections emphasizing dental benefits, product features, and health positioning

hierarchyStructure.distribution: Image-heavy strategy with 223 editorial images distributed across pages, averaging 8.9 images per page

brandIdentity.visualLanguage: Clean, professional aesthetic with focus on product excellence and dental benefits. Bright, trustworthy photography emphasizing health outcomes. Oral-B branding and product imagery prominently featured. All-caps category and sub-category naming (Elektrische Zahnbürsten, Aufsteckbürsten, Stiftung Warentest) establishes authority and clarity.
brandIdentity.targetAudience: Health-conscious families, individuals seeking professional dental care, consumers with specific dental concerns (sensitivity, gum health, whitening), both adults and children
brandIdentity.navigationPhilosophy: Dual-axis navigation combining product-type organization (Elektrische Zahnbürsten, Aufsteckbürsten, Zahnpasta & Zahnseide) with need-state/benefit shopping (Shoppen nach Bedarf: Tiefenreinigung, Zahnfleischgesundheit, Empfindliche Zähne, Whitening). This hybrid approach allows customers to find products both by what they are and what problem they solve. Deep specialization across product lines (iO Series, PRO Series) with family-specific variants (Kids models). Credibility reinforced through Stiftung Warentest page and clinical positioning.
brandIdentity.contentStrategy: Extremely image-heavy strategy (223 editorial images, 4 videos) with particular emphasis on need-state pages (60 images across 4 benefit-focused pages). Minimal video usage focuses on product demonstrations and new feature highlights. Large product category overview pages (Zahnpasta & Zahnseide with 18 images) showcase complete range. This positions Oral-B as comprehensive solution provider for all oral health needs rather than simple product vendor.

comparisonNotes.versus_ESN: Oral-B uses 223 editorial images (vs ESN's 75) - 3x more image-heavy approach reflecting different industries. Oral-B emphasizes 25 pages (vs ESN's 21) with focus on need-state shopping, while ESN emphasizes deep sub-brand specialization. Oral-B has minimal video usage (4 videos vs ESN's 6) despite much larger image inventory. Color schemes differ - Oral-B uses professional healthcare whites vs ESN's black/white elite positioning. Both use 2-level navigation but serve different purposes: Oral-B enables shopping by benefit or product type; ESN presents sub-brands as distinct ecosystems.
